stdout:
I’m switching to read-only tooling and sub-agents since direct shell access is blocked here. Next I’m gathering the changed-file set, the design requirements, and an independent diff analysis in parallel.

I’ve got the design criteria loaded. While a diff-inspection agent maps the changed hunks, I’m starting the required validation suite so the review covers both code quality and acceptance criteria.

The diff is tiny, but one change looks potentially problematic. I’m pulling the exact file context now so the review can cite precise line-level evidence instead of relying on summaries.

I’ve confirmed the exact hunk and test coverage. One more quick pass is to check whether the server already has a stable project identity source; if not, that strengthens the finding against using `current_dir()` in `/health`.

I’ve got enough to make the call, but I’m doing one last evidence sweep: checking whether anything in the web app actually consumes the new `/health.project_name`, and whether the diff covers any of the epic’s required preset/state-transition surfaces at all.

I found a bigger acceptance-criteria concern: the production-polish epic calls for web mutation APIs and UI transition controls, and I’m not seeing those surfaces at all. I’m checking the specific server and web entry points now so the review can cite the missing implementation precisely.

## Acceptance criteria

**Fail.** The epic’s design requires web mutation support and UI transition controls, but the current tree still lacks them.

`crates/server/src/routes.rs:29-56` exposes only `GET` routes. The required mutation endpoints from the design (`POST /issues/:id/transition`, `/claim`, `/gates/run`, `/issues/bulk`) are absent.

On the frontend, `web/src/api/client.ts:15-39` only defines read methods, and `web/src/components/Issue/IssueDetail.tsx:241-266` plus `:508-567` only provide “Focus in Graph” and gate-history viewing, not “Start Work”, “Submit for Review”, “Mark Done”, claim controls, or gate-run triggers.

## Architecture and separation of concerns

No domain/storage boundary violation is introduced by the diff itself; changes stay in `crates/server` and `web/`.

However, `crates/server/src/routes.rs:61-64` derives `project_name` from `std::env::current_dir()` inside the request handler. That makes the API response depend on process environment rather than explicit app state/config. `crates/server/src/main.rs:25-31,57-79` already has a startup-time `data_dir` source that would be a more appropriate boundary.

## Functional paradigm

No major functional-style regression in the small diff. This area is acceptable.

## Correctness and invariants

`crates/server/src/routes.rs:61-64` is a blocking correctness issue. `/health` now returns `project_name` based on the server process CWD basename, with a silent fallback to `"jit"`.

That is unreliable in common deployments where the working directory is `/`, a service directory, or otherwise unrelated to the tracked repo. It also leaks deployment-path information from an unauthenticated health endpoint. Because `web/src/App.tsx:73-76` uses this value for `document.title`, the UI can display the wrong repository identity.

No atomic-write, event-log, gate-semantic, label-format, assignee-format, or PID-safety regressions were found in the changed lines.

## Safety

No `unsafe` code observed. `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets` passed.

## Testing

Validation passed:

`./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passed with label warnings only.

`cargo test --workspace` passed.

`cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets` passed.

`cargo fmt --all -- --check` passed.

But the new health-path behavior is under-tested. `crates/server/src/routes.rs:741-749` only asserts that `project_name` is a string, so it would not catch the wrong-name and `/` fallback cases introduced by `current_dir()`.

## Error handling

`crates/server/src/routes.rs:61-64` silently converts resolution failure into `"jit"`. That masks misconfiguration instead of surfacing or avoiding the unreliable field entirely.

## CLI contract

No CLI surface was changed. Nothing blocking here.

## Documentation

No user-facing documentation or API documentation was updated for the new `/health.project_name` response field.

## Prior review feedback

`run_history` is empty, so there is no prior review feedback to verify.

## Jit issue management

Issue dependencies in the provided context are satisfied (done or rejected as appropriate).

`./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passed; the reported label warnings are allowed per the review instructions.

VERDICT: FAIL
---
Gate result: FAILED
